codeReview.summary: The coins refactor is mostly a structural extraction, but it introduced a behavioral regression in top-gainers/losers query parsing that is not covered by the retained characterization suite.

issues.description: parseMoverPriceChangePercentage no longer uses the shared CSV parser and now silently drops blank entries. Requests like `/coins/top_gainers_losers?vs_currency=usd&price_change_percentage=24h,` previously raised `invalid_parameter` because parseCsvQuery removed the empty token and the legacy guard rejected the resulting empty list; after the refactor this helper returns `["24h"]`, changing the HTTP contract for malformed input during a behavior-preserving refactor.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The safe-refactor skill requires tightening characterization tests before production refactors, but this worker relied on existing coverage and missed malformed top-movers CSV input semantics. The procedure would be safer if it explicitly called out invalid-parameter characterization for any extracted parser helpers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 2 says to write or tighten characterization tests before changing code. Handoff says `no new tests were required`, while the refactor changed `parseMoverPriceChangePercentage` in `src/modules/coins/helpers.ts:167-178` from shared CSV parsing to manual splitting and no test covers `/coins/top_gainers_losers?...price_change_percentage=24h,` in `tests/app.test.ts` or `tests/invalid-params.test.ts`.

summary: Fail: the refactor drifted malformed `price_change_percentage` handling for `/coins/top_gainers_losers`, so it does not fully preserve previously validated route behavior.
